Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Finding the right talent just got easier with SmartHirePress enter or click to view image in full size

One dashboard, the whole pipeline — nothing buried in a spreadsheetA few months ago, I sat with a friend who runs talent acquisition for a mid-sized tech company. She had 400 applications open on her screen for a single role. Not 400 good candidates — 400 applications, most of them irrelevant, keyword-stuffed, or just plain wrong for the job. She was going to spend her entire weekend just getting through the pile.
That’s not recruiting. That’s triage.
And she’s not alone. Ask almost any HR person in India right now what their week actually looks like, and you’ll hear some version of the same story: too many resumes, too little signal, and a job board that’s great at generating volume but not particularly great at helping you find the right person fast.
That’s the gap we built SmartHire to close.
What SmartHire Actually Does
We’re not trying to be another resume database. India already has excellent ones — Naukri, Shine, and a handful of others have spent years building massive candidate pools, and honestly, they’re good at what they do.
What we noticed was different: almost nobody was solving the problem after the resumes land in your inbox. The screening. The ranking. The “which of these 400 people should I actually talk to first” problem.
So SmartHire is built around that moment — the AI does the heavy lifting of reading, understanding, and ranking candidates against what the role actually needs, not just keyword overlap. It’s not a black box either. If you’re going to trust a system to help decide who gets a callback, you should be able to see why it made that call. That mattered a lot to us, especially given how much scrutiny AI hiring tools are (rightly) getting right now.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Candidates ranked by JD fit — recruiters see the score, not just a name in a pileMeet Smarty
The part of the product I’m most proud of is probably the one that’s easiest to explain badly, so let me try to explain it simply.
You paste in a job description. That’s it. Smarty takes it from there — it goes and finds matching candidates, scores every single one against what you actually asked for, and queues up personalized outreach to the best matches. What used to be three separate steps (source, screen, reach out), each eating a chunk of someone’s day, happens as one pipeline.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Paste a JD, set your thresholds, and let Smarty do the sourcingWe didn’t want a magic black box, though — you can see the threshold Smarty is scoring against, adjust it, and see exactly how many candidates cleared the bar before anything gets sent.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

20 sourced, 20 shortlisted, 20 queued for outreach — one JD, one passFor a recruiter who used to spend a full day just getting through the sourcing step, that’s the whole thing done before their coffee’s cold.
Why We Built It for India, Specifically
A lot of AI recruiting tools are built somewhere else and dropped into the Indian market with a currency converter slapped on. We didn’t want to do that.
That meant thinking about things global tools usually skip — regional language support, pricing that actually makes sense in INR instead of an awkward dollar conversion, and workflows that fit how Indian teams actually hire, from high-volume BFSI recruiting to fast-moving startup teams in Bangalore and Hyderabad hiring five roles at once.
Where We’re At
Press enter or click to view image in full size

A real hiring pipeline inside SmartHire, from shortlist to hiredWe’re early. We’re not claiming to have solved recruiting — nobody has, and I’d be skeptical of anyone who says they have. But every week we talk to more HR teams who are tired of spending their time sorting resumes instead of actually talking to people, and that’s the problem we’re staying focused on.
If that sounds like your Monday morning too, I’d genuinely love to hear how your team is handling it right now — and if you want to see what SmartHire looks like in practice, come say hello.

If you’re building or scaling a hiring team and want to swap notes on what’s actually working (and what isn’t) in AI recruiting, drop a comment or reach out — always happy to talk shop with fellow HR folks.
